items.title: Confronting images of 'unprecedented' outback flood after cyclone

children.content: An outback cattle station and tourist ranch in WA's north has felt the full force of Cyclone Narelle, with vision emerging of the trail of destruction left by the powerful wind and heavy rain.

image.altText: What appears to be a goat seeks shelter in a car while being pummelled with fast moving flood water.

gallery.altText: A man paddleboards through a rural property

thumbnail.altText: A man paddleboards through a rural property

items.title: Emergency relief announced as cleanup begins for ex-tropical Cyclone Narelle

image.altText: A worker is repairing electricity transmission lines.

children.content: Structures have been destroyed and more than 1000 people remain without power as the cleanup begins in Western Australia following ex-tropical cyclone Narelle.

items.title: Consumer pain within 'weeks' if WA diesel supplies aren't resolved

children.content: Country business owners say they have worked to absorb price increases, but will be forced to pass on costs to consumers if the supply of fuel to regional WA isn't stabilised.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a hand holding a full pint of beer below a beer tap after pouring it at a bar in a pub.

items.title: Vital Aboriginal records saved in the 70s by an astute WA archivist

children.content: Margaret Medcalf, who turns 100 this week, insisted that decades of Aboriginal welfare records be preserved in the state archives and not destroyed. Those documents have proved essential to connecting Aboriginal people with their family history.

image.altText: black and white photo of Margaret Medcalf in the archives in 1969

items.title: Cyclone Narelle destroys banana crops, fresh food growers hit hard

children.content: Fruit and vegetable producers in one of Western Australia's key food growing regions are counting the cost of Cyclone Narelle, with one banana grower reporting more than 80 per cent of his crop has been destroyed.

gallery.altText: A wide shot of a man wearing work uniform and walking through his banana plantation.
Green bananas are seen falling off the ground in a plantation.
A man looks up at his banana plantation with a sad look on his face.

thumbnail.altText: A wide shot of a man wearing work uniform and walking through his banana plantation.
Green bananas are seen falling off the ground in a plantation.
A man looks up at his banana plantation with a sad look on his face.

items.title: Haggis and traditional dancing in WA's outback

children.content: Scottish expats in WA's outback host traditional Ceilidh in a belated honouring of Burns Night.

image.altText: three girls smiling in front of a scottish flag

items.title: Man charged after baby killed, woman and child injured as rideshare car hits tree

children.content: A seven-month-old child has died after a rideshare vehicle crashed into a tree in Perth's south-east, leaving two other passengers seriously injured.

image.altText: Lynwood crash

items.title: After 80 years, sleuths have unearthed a secret stash of early art and reunited them with family

children.content: A stash of more than 800 mysterious drawings has been revealed for the first time, rewriting the timeline of Aboriginal art and reconnecting families with long-lost relatives.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of men sitting in the dirt drawing on paper
Red and blue shapes with a red bird in the top left
A man wearing a hat and dark glasses sits on the ground amid burning scrub.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of men sitting in the dirt drawing on paper
Red and blue shapes with a red bird in the top left
A man wearing a hat and dark glasses sits on the ground amid burning scrub.

items.title: No transport relief for Sydneysiders as fuel prices surge

children.content: The NSW Liberals and the Rail, Tram and Bus Union (RTBU) have renewed calls for free public transport in response to surging fuel prices.

articles.title: Free public transport in Tasmania for three months to counter fuel crisis

children.content: The Tasmanian government says public transport will be free statewide for three months, starting on Monday, in response to surging fuel prices.

image.altText: Metro bus

articles.title: Public transport to be free for Victorians in April as fuel prices climb

items.title: Middle East war has another new front and Trump only wanted one of them

children.content: One month into the Middle East conflict, the fighting has expanded again with another new front opening up as the Houthis launched their first attack. It creates further complexity for Donald Trump who now has to contain a widening conflict, writes Allyson Horn.

items.title: EPA, developer defend records over contentious WA beach development

items.title: The extraordinary story of Australia's oldest living Olympian

children.content: Gordon "Wingnut" Ingate was twice denied time off work to compete in the Olympic Games in his 20s, but continued to excel as a sailor, eventually getting the chance to compete in his 40s at Munich 1972.
